title: Anaphylaxis / Severe Allergic Reaction
source: American Academy of Allergy, Asthma & Immunology / AHA

slides.title: Use EpiPen Immediately
slides.content: If an epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen, Auvi-Q) is available: Remove safety cap. Press firmly against the outer thigh — it can go through clothing. Hold for 10 seconds. Note the exact time of injection.
slides.warning: Antihistamines (Benadryl, Zyrtec) are NOT sufficient for anaphylaxis. Epinephrine is the ONLY effective first-line treatment. Do not delay for any reason.
slides.tts_text: Inject the EpiPen into the outer thigh immediately. Hold for 10 seconds. Note the time.

slides.title: Call 911
slides.content: Call 911 immediately — even if the EpiPen was used. Epinephrine wears off in 15–20 minutes. Biphasic reactions (return of symptoms) occur in up to 20% of cases. Hospital observation is required.
slides.tts_text: Call 911 immediately. EpiPen buys time — emergency care is still required.

slides.title: Position the Person
slides.content: Lay flat on their back with legs elevated about 12 inches. If breathing is easier sitting up, let them sit. Do not allow them to stand or walk — this can cause cardiovascular collapse.
slides.warning: If vomiting or unconscious: place on their side (recovery position). If not breathing and no pulse: begin CPR.
slides.tts_text: Lay the person flat with legs elevated. Let them sit if breathing is easier. Never let them stand.

slides.title: Remove the Trigger
slides.content: If bee or wasp sting: scrape the stinger out sideways (don't squeeze — squeezing injects more venom). Remove any remaining food. Move away from chemical exposure.
slides.tts_text: If bee sting, scrape the stinger out. Remove any food or exposure trigger.

slides.title: Second EpiPen if Needed
slides.content: If symptoms do not improve or worsen within 5–15 minutes AND a second auto-injector is available: inject into the opposite thigh.
slides.tts_text: If no improvement in 5 to 15 minutes and a second EpiPen is available, use it in the opposite thigh.

slides.title: Monitor Continuously
slides.content: Stay with the person. Monitor breathing, consciousness, and skin color every 1–2 minutes. Be prepared to start CPR. Keep them warm if possible.
slides.tts_text: Stay with the person. Monitor breathing and consciousness every minute. Be ready to start CPR.

slides.title: Wait for EMS — Do Not Leave
slides.content: Do not let the person leave or drive. Even if they feel completely better, hospital evaluation is mandatory. Biphasic reactions can occur 1–72 hours later.

completion.message: Keep the person calm and still. EMS is on the way. Hospital care is required — do not let them go home.

slides.tts_text: Keep the person calm and still. Emergency services are coming. Do not let them go home. Hospital care is required.
